reasoning: The obfuscated question "Will Z.ai have the top AI model on December 31?" refers to xAI, based on Polymarket odds from June 2025 which had xAI at a 20% chance to top the leaderboard by Dec 31. Since then, xAI released Grok 4 in July 2025, which only reached the #3/#4 spot, trailing behind Google's Gemini 2.5 Pro and OpenAI's o3. Furthermore, the landscape became even more competitive in August 2025 with the launch event for GPT-5, during which Google's prediction market odds actually jumped, suggesting Google is currently seen as the dominant frontrunner. Because Grok 4 failed to capture the #1 spot upon release and AI competitors have already deployed their next-generation models (Gemini 2.5, GPT-5, o3), xAI's chances of holding the #1 spot on Dec 31 have diminished compared to June. Unless xAI launches a surprise Grok 5 in late Q4 that surpasses all competitors, a win is unlikely. Therefore, I estimate xAI's probability has dropped to around 8%.
